note: We both need core.py, true file-disjointness isn't possible here. Proposal: split by section instead. I will ONLY touch: Option.__init__ (append new batch_size kwarg at the end of the signature, add validation in the existing __debug__ block), Option.to_info_dict (append a batch_size key), and the multiple-value processing path (Parameter.type_cast_value / a new batching helper) plus a small addition in types.py if needed for validation. I will NOT touch Parameter.__init__'s default-checking logic, get_default(), consume_value(), lookup_default(), or Context.default_map handling - that sounds like your area for dynamic default resolution. Please avoid touching Option.__init__'s attrs after the existing count-related init and to_info_dict's batch_size key. If that works, ACCEPT and we'll each implement in our own non-overlapping regions.

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Confirmed no overlap. My batch_size edits, all scoped to the Option class only: (1) Option.__init__ signature - add new 'batch_size: int | None = None' kwarg appended after existing kwargs, store self.batch_size, validation in the existing __debug__ block (TypeError if batch_size set but multiple is False, ValueError if batch_size < 0); (2) Option.to_info_dict - append batch_size=self.batch_size; (3) a NEW override method Option.type_cast_value(ctx, value) that calls super().type_cast_value() then chunks the resulting tuple into sub-tuples of batch_size when set. I will NOT touch Parameter.__init__, Parameter.type_cast_value, get_default, consume_value, get_help_record, or Command.main - all yours. types.py: turns out I don't need it either (no shared ParamType logic required), so feel free to disregard - dropping it from my claimed files, core.py only for both of us now. Go ahead and implement; I'll do the same and we'll each run git diff independently at the end.
